page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-16xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The CPU configuration includes a 60 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations.

The server is equipped with 512.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7iz.metal-16xl demonstrates strong performance profiles. It achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Database operations via Passmark and Redis SET operations also rank highly, with Redis reaching 85.67 million operations per second. The hardware delivers strong performance in LLM inference tasks, particularly for prompt processing with medium-sized models. This high resource density and DDR5 memory speed offer qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads, though the lack of local storage requires external storage provisioning.

Given its high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and substantial DDR5 memory capacity, the r7iz.metal-16xl is optimized for memory-intensive and compute-heavy workloads. It is highly suited for relational databases, in-memory data stores, software compilation, data processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-16xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processors running at 3.9 GHz. Equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, this instance provides 8.0 GB of RAM per core but contains no local storage or GPUs. It supports a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, single-threaded compression, and database operations, alongside strong performance in Redis SET operations and LLM inference. The server is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory data stores, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.

tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized bare-metal server with DDR5 RAM and advanced matrix extensions for demanding database and compute workloads.
